articles.title: Commercial LPG cylinder prices slashed by Rs 183.50 from July 1: Check city-wise rates
articles.description: Commercial LPG prices have seen a significant drop of Rs 183.50 effective July 1, bringing relief to businesses like hotels and vendors. A 19 kg cylinder now costs Rs 2930 in Delhi.  Meanwhile, prices for household cylinders remain unchanged. This follows a price hike in June attributed to supply issues.

articles.title: 'The numbers don't add up': Karnataka minister grills officials over stray dogs, missing trees; watch
articles.description: Bengaluru's development minister, Krishna Byre Gowda, expressed dismay over the ineffectiveness of the Animal Birth Control program, questioning how Rs 100 crore spent has yielded only marginal reduction in stray dogs. He also demanded answers regarding the disappearance of over 11 lakh saplings planted in two decades. Officials struggled to provide satisfactory explanations, prompting the minister to call for scaled-up sterilization and transparent tracking of tree plantation survival rates.

articles.description: The Board of School Education, Haryana has released the HTET 2026 admit cards for candidates appearing in the July 4–5 teacher eligibility examination. Registered applicants can now access their hall tickets online using their login credentials. Candidates should verify all details on the admit card, as it is mandatory for entry into the examination centre.

articles.title: TNEA rank list 2026 expected to be released today at tneaonline.org: Check complete details here
articles.description: The TNEA Rank List 2026 is scheduled to be released on July 1 after being postponed from June 29 to incorporate revised CBSE re-evaluation marks. Prepared on a standardized 200-mark scale, the merit list will determine candidates' eligibility and ranking for engineering counselling and seat allotment across Tamil Nadu. Candidates can check their ranks on the official TNEA portal.

articles.title: CBSE Class 10 second board exam results soon: What is the best marks rule students must know?
articles.description: CBSE's Better Marks Rule, introduced under the new two-board examination system for Class 10 from the 2026 academic session, allows students to retain the higher score from two board exam attempts. Applied separately to each subject, the rule offers a chance to improve marks or clear failed papers while reducing exam pressure and eliminating the risk of losing existing scores.

articles.title: CBSE Class 12 supplementary exam 2026 announced; Schools asked to submit LOC by July 8
articles.description: CBSE has begun the registration process for the Class 12 Supplementary Examination 2026, directing affiliated schools to submit the List of Candidates (LOC) through the Pariksha Sangam portal between June 30 and July 8. Eligible compartment and improvement candidates can apply, while students omitted from the LOC will not be permitted to appear for the examination.

articles.title: Akshay on 'Welcome 3: 'Friday still feels like an exam result'
articles.description: Akshay Kumar expressed his gratitude for 'Welcome to the Jungle's box office success with a simple 'Jai Mahakal!' He debunked the myth of a secret film-producing machine, emphasizing discipline and time management. Despite decades in the industry, Kumar admitted to still experiencing pre-release jitters, viewing each Friday as an exam. He also revealed that his current nervousness is more for his co-stars and crew, wishing them the best.

articles.title: SS Rajamouli gives big 'Varanasi' update
articles.description: Filmmaker SS Rajamouli's ambitious project 'Varanasi,' starring Mahesh Babu, Priyanka Chopra, and Prithviraj Sukumaran, is nearing completion with approximately 80 days of shooting remaining. The film, a time-traveling action adventure, has already wrapped major action sequences. A massive war episode involving thousands of junior artists is scheduled in Hyderabad, marking a significant part of the grand production. 'Varanasi' is slated for a 2027 release.

articles.title: Melissa Gilbert reflects on child stardom after Daveigh Chase tribute.
articles.description: Actress Melissa Gilbert mourns the loss of former child co-star Daveigh Chase, 35, who passed away from AIDS and malnutrition. Gilbert's tribute highlights the perils child actors face, citing Chase's struggles with addiction and homelessness. She urges parents to prioritize their child's well-being and ensure a balanced life outside the industry, using Chase's tragic story as a stark warning.

articles.title: Bollywood's biggest shocks in the first half of 2026
articles.description: Bollywood has always thrived on surprises, but the first half of 2026 took things to another level. The year was packed with headlines that left fans stunned, sparked endless debates on social media and dominated entertainment news cycles for weeks. From unexpected wedding announcements and heartwarming pregnancy news to legal battles, industry controversies and the loss of a legendary icon, the Hindi film industry experienced moments that will be remembered for years to come.
articles.full_text: Bollywood has always thrived on surprises, but the first half of 2026 took things to another level. The year was packed with headlines that left fans stunned, sparked endless debates on social media and dominated entertainment news cycles for weeks. From unexpected wedding announcements and heartwarming pregnancy news to legal battles, industry controversies and the loss of a legendary icon, the Hindi film industry experienced moments that will be remembered for years to come. One of the year's happiest surprises came from Aamir Khan, who announced that he would be marrying his longtime partner, Gauri Spratt. After months of public appearances together and growing speculation about their relationship, the superstar confirmed the news, delighting fans across the country. The couple chose to keep the celebrations understated, opting for an intimate registered marriage at their home on July 5, attended only by close family members and friends. Just a couple of years after embracing motherhood, Deepika Padukone gave fans another reason to celebrate by announcing that she and husband Ranveer Singh are expecting their second child. The couple shared the news on April 19 through a touching joint Instagram post featuring their daughter, Dua Padukone Singh, holding a positive pregnancy test while both parents lovingly held her hands. The emotional announcement quickly went viral, with fans and celebrities flooding social media with congratulatory messages. Deepika and Ranveer, who tied the knot in Italy's Lake Como in 2018, welcomed daughter Dua in September 2024. Their second pregnancy announcement became one of the most heartwarming celebrity moments of 2026. Rajpal Yadav found himself in the headlines after surrendering at Delhi's Tihar Jail to serve a six-month sentence in a long-running cheque bounce and loan default case. The dispute dated back to 2010, when Yadav borrowed Rs 5 crore from Delhi-based Murali Projects Pvt Ltd to finance his directorial venture Ata Pata Laapata. Following the film's commercial failure and years of non-payment, the outstanding amount reportedly escalated to nearly Rs 9 crore. After the Delhi High Court dismissed his requests for additional time to repay the dues, the actor surrendered in February 2026. However, he secured interim bail after spending 12 days in jail by depositing Rs 1.5 crore directly into the complainant's account and furnishing a personal bond. Oscar-winning composer AR Rahman found himself at the centre of a major controversy after suggesting that a subtle "communal thing" could be among the reasons for his reduced workload in Hindi cinema. Speaking about changing industry dynamics, Rahman claimed that he was often informed through "Chinese whispers" that projects initially meant for him eventually went to multiple other composers. His comments generated intense debate across social media and the film fraternity, with supporters and critics offering sharply different interpretations. As criticism mounted, Rahman clarified that his remarks had been misunderstood and insisted that he never intended to hurt anyone's sentiments. He reiterated his love for the country and later said he had moved on from the episode, but the controversy remained one of the year's biggest talking points. Not every headline was celebratory. One of the biggest industry controversies erupted after Ranveer Singh's sudden exit from Don 3. The fallout intensified when producer-director Farhan Akhtar's Excel Entertainment alleged that the actor's withdrawal had resulted in nearly Rs 45 crore in pre-production losses. The dispute prompted the Federation of Western India Cine Employees (FWICE) to issue a Non-Cooperation Directive against Ranveer. Although several reports initially described the move as an industry ban, FWICE later clarified that it was not a legal ban but an advisory requesting its members not to work with the actor until the dispute was resolved. Ranveer's representatives and legal experts maintained that the industry body had no statutory authority over a private commercial disagreement. Businessman and former IPL chairman Lalit Modi returned to the spotlight after speaking publicly about his past relationship with actress Sushmita Sen. Addressing the criticism the actress faced after they made their relationship public in 2022, Modi strongly defended Sen against the "gold digger" label that had circulated online. In a candid conversation, he described her as an independent, self-made and financially successful woman. He even joked that she rarely allowed him to pay for anything during their relationship and humorously referred to himself as the "diamond digger." Modi also clarified that the relationship ended because of geographical distance rather than any controversy, adding that they continue to remain good friends. The most emotional moment of 2026 came with the passing of legendary singer Asha Bhosle on April 12, bringing the curtain down on one of the greatest musical careers in Indian history. Tributes poured in from across the globe as musicians, actors, filmmakers and millions of fans celebrated a remarkable legacy that spanned more than seven decades. Following a public homage at her Mumbai residence, the nation gathered to bid farewell to the iconic singer. Her final journey was marked with full state honours, with her mortal remains draped in the Indian tricolour and a ceremonial gun salute by the Mumbai Police. She was cremated at Shivaji Park in Mumbai, where thousands assembled to pay their last respects. Her son, Anand Bhosle, performed the final rites in an emotional ceremony attended by members of the Mangeshkar family, political leaders and colleagues from the film industry. Her passing marked the end of an era, leaving behind an unmatched musical legacy that will continue to inspire generations.
